title: Crumbling Codex
author: Krümblegård
pages:
§6§lKrümblegård

§7A Fractured World Awaits

§6§lWayfall

§7A tear in reality.

§aWayfall§7 is a shattered void of
floating islands, living stone,
and reactive terrain.

§7Progress here leads to §5Runic craft§7…
and something far worse.

§6§lEntering Wayfall

§7• §aWaystone§7 (rare first-use pull)
§7• §aWayfall Portal§7 (Creative)

§8────────────

§7You will always land safely
on a spawn island.

§8Hold §lShift§r§8 to resist a Waystone pull.

§6§lFirst Steps

§7• Gather §aFault Dust
§7• Harvest §aRunegrowth
§7• Cut §aWayfall wood
§7• Farm §aPaleweft Corn§7 → §aWeftkern

§8Dormant stone may be §5Traprock§8.

§6§lFood & Survival

§aPaleweft§7 grows across Wayfall.

§7Secure a steady §aWeftkern§7 supply
before pushing deeper.

§8If you can farm,
you can survive.

§6§lMobility

§aScaralon Beetles§7 can be tamed
and ridden.

§7• Ground sprint
§7• Powered flight
§7• Egg laying

§l§7Elytra are renewable here.§r

§5§lRunic Path

§7Runic Debris
§8↓
§7Smelt → Runic Scrap
§8↓
§74× Scrap + 4× Copper
§8↓
§7Runic Ingot
§8↓
§7Netherite + Runic
§8↓
§5Attuned Ingot

§5§lThe Warning

§7Something rules Wayfall.

§7It has phases.

§7It remembers you.

§3§lCephalari Species Codex
§7Wayfall Stranded Variant

§8Sapient nautiloid-descended
survivors of a dimensional rupture.

§7A people defined by amnesia,
adaptation, and survival.

§3§l1. Forgotten Origins

§7The Cephalari came from an
unknown dimension. Their arrival
in Wayfall was catastrophic: a
rupture that shattered reality and
destroyed their memory-shell
archives.

§3§l1. Forgotten Origins

§7Only instinctive fragments remain:
§7• Spiral motifs in art & engineering
§7• A biological expectation of buoyancy
§7• Pressure-based logic instincts
§7• A cultural ache for a home
  they cannot name

§3§l2. Physiology in Wayfall

§7Cephalari bodies were never meant
for land. Their original biology
relied on drifting and buoyancy,
not walking.

§3§l2.1 Morphology

§7• Spiral shell reinforced with
  Wayfall crystalline dust
§7• 20–40 tentacles: fine work + bracing
§7• Internal pressure sacs
§7• Chromatophores glow in air
§7• Blink-reflex short dodges

§3§l2.2 Environmental Needs

§7• No longer require water
§7• Thrive only in Wayfall’s air
§7• Suffocate slowly in overworld-type air
§7• Immune to fall damage (pressure sacs
  and blink reflex)

§3§l2.3 Lifespan

§7• 180–260 years
§7• Memory degradation is common
  due to dimensional trauma

§3§l3. Mount-Bound Biology

§7Cephalari cannot move efficiently
on their own. To survive in Wayfall,
they rely on scavenged-tech mobility
mounts built from fragments of their
forgotten dimension and Wayfall debris.

§3§lMount Rules

§7• Every adult Cephalari spawns with
  one mount
§7• Mounts despawn with the Cephalari
§7• Mounts are extensions of the body
§7• Each mount reflects a survival strategy

§3§l4.1 Spiral Strider

§7A multi-legged walker built from
shell fragments and salvaged pistons.

§7Role: height, stability, terrain mastery
§7Movement: slow, extremely stable
§7Symbolism: continuity, spiral memory

§3§l4.2 Drift Skimmer

§7A hovering manta-shaped craft that
glides using membrane wings and
pressure-vent propulsion.

§7Role: speed, long traversal
§7Movement: smooth gliding + bursts
§7Symbolism: echoes aquatic ancestry

§3§l4.3 Treadwinder Chassis

§7A compact armored rig on spiral-linked
tank treads.

§7Role: everyday mobility, rugged terrain
§7Movement: smooth rolling + pivot
§7Symbolism: resilience and ingenuity

§8The iconic default mount.

§3§l4.4 Echo Harness

§7A semi-organic exosuit the Cephalari
“slots into,” becoming a tall armored
walker.

§7Role: combat, defense
§7Movement: stride + blink-dash + jumps
§7Symbolism: fused identity & survival

§3§l5. Society and Culture

§7Tide-Kin clusters form around:
§7• shell pattern motifs
§7• mount type
§7• shared memory fragments

§7Elder Spirals preserve what remains.

§3§l5.3 Values

§7• Adaptation
§7• Continuity
§7• Symbiosis with mounts
§7• Stability in a hostile dimension

§3§l6. Wayfall Villages

§7Cephalari villages are rumored to
exist only in Wayfall.

§7Architecture:
§7• Spiral platforms on coral fibers
§7• Bio-ceramic fused with crystal
§7• Stabilized chambers
§7• Memory Gardens
§7• Mount platforms

§3§l6.2 Village Layout

§7• Central Spiral Hall
§7• Ceramic Kilns
§7• Flow Engine Chambers
§7• Crystal Beds
§7• Scent Archives
§7• Mount Bays

§3§l7. Professions

§7Cephalari work revolves around
stabilizing Wayfall and maintaining
mounts.

§7• Ceramist
§7• Flowwright
§7• Crystal Gardener
§7• Shellsmith
§7• Archivist
§7• Navigator

§3§l8. Threats and Predators

§7Wayfall is hostile. Villages endure:
§7• Wraithlike distortive entities
§7• Riftborn Cephalari (corrupted)
§7• Gravity-adapted predators
§7• Spatial instability events

§8Mounts are essential for survival.

§3§l9. Subspecies

§7• Sky-Pelagic — gliders, bright shells
§7• Deepfall — bioluminescent,
  distortion-resistant
§7• Riftborn — corrupted, hostile,
  red-gold shells

§3§l10.1 Zombified Cephalari

§7When a Cephalari zombifies:
§7• Its original mount does NOT appear
§7• It spawns riding a zombie-type mob
§7• The zombie mount persists if the
  Cephalari dies

§8Conversion cinematic: no Zoglins.

§3§lEligible Zombie Mounts

§7• Zombie
§7• Drowned
§7• Husk
§7• Zombified Piglin
§7• Zoglin (natural spawns only)
§7• Other zombie-type mobs (world-dependent)

§3§l10.2 Conversion Back

§7When a zombified Cephalari is cured:
§7• The Cephalari returns to normal
§7• The zombie mount is removed
§7• The stored Cephalari mount type is
  restored

§7Zoglins cannot convert and are
replaced like any other zombie mount.

§3§l11. Player Interaction

§7Travelers who find Cephalari may:
§7• Trade for Wayfall materials
§7• Learn pressure-logic practices
§7• Acquire shell-based armor
§7• Defend Tide-Kin from Riftborn raids
§7• Navigate unstable terrain

§8Safe havens in a hostile dimension.
